reason: Response B sustains a tighter, more controlled comic logic with restraint and a strong escalating payoff (the "you are always watching" twist), which reflects the steadier craft typical of the more capable Sonnet tier. Response A is funnier in places but scattershot, piling on gags (unions, Library of Alexandria, Swiss watch) in a more is-more spray that reads like the smaller Haiku model reaching for volume over coherence. The disciplined structure and satisfying closing reversal in B tip me toward it being Sonnet.
